title: hieracium murorum subsp. atrocinereum
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. atrocinereum is a subspecies of the Hawkweed plant, characterized by its small yellow flowers and a rosette of leaves. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and sesquiterpenes. The plant is believed to have health benefits, including its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Hieracium murorum subsp. atrocinereum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
